Primary Endpoint(s).value: The primary outcome was overall survival, defined as the time from randomization to death from any cause. The intermediate primary outcome was failure-free survival (treatment failure was defined as radiologic, clinical, or PSA progression or death from prostate cancer).

The intermediate primary outcome was failure-free survival, defined as the time to the first of the following forms of treatment failure: biochemical (prostate-specific antigen [PSA]) failure (see the protocol, available with the full text of this article at NEJM.org); progression of local, lymph-node, or distant metastases; or death from prostate cancer.'

Type of Therapy.value: Androgen-deprivation therapy (ADT) alone or ADT plus abiraterone acetate (1000 mg daily) and prednisolone (5 mg daily) (combination therapy). Local radiotherapy was also mandated for patients with node-negative, nonmetastatic disease and encouraged for those with positive nodes.
Type of Therapy.reasoning: The ABSTRACT section on page 1 states: "We randomly assigned patients in a 1:1 ratio to receive ADT alone or ADT plus abiraterone acetate (1000 mg daily) and prednisolone (5 mg daily) (combination therapy). Local radiotherapy was mandated for patients with node-negative, nonmetastatic disease and encouraged for those with positive nodes." The METHODS section on page 2 further defines ADT as "orchiectomy or gonadotropin-releasing hormone [GnRH] agonists or antagonists." The PROCEDURES section on page 3 reiterates these treatment components.
